advanced.question: What is declarationMap?
advanced.explanation: Enables "Go to Definition" to navigate to the original TS source.

advanced.question: What is sourceMap?
advanced.explanation: Source maps map compiled JS back to TS for debugging with browser tools.

advanced.question: What is terser or minification in TS?
options: External tool; TS does not minify output
advanced.explanation: Use Terser or esbuild for minification; tsc only transpiles.

advanced.question: What is the satisfies operator used for?
advanced.explanation: const x = { a: "hello" } satisfies Record<string, string> keeps "hello" type.

advanced.question: What is Variance in generics?
options: How generic types relate through subtyping
advanced.explanation: TS 4.7+ supports optional variance annotations using in/out modifiers.

advanced.question: What does the in modifier mean?
options: Generic parameter is used only in input positions
advanced.explanation: in means contravariant (e.g., function parameter).

advanced.question: What does the out modifier mean?
options: Generic parameter is used only in output positions
advanced.explanation: out means covariant (e.g., return type).

advanced.question: What is const type parameter?
options: Generic parameter inferred as literal type
advanced.explanation: function get<T extends const>(val: T) infers exact literal values.

advanced.question: What is the satisfies keyword?
options: Checks type match without widening to base type
advanced.explanation: satisfies keeps narrow types while ensuring they match a broader type.

advanced.question: What is the using declaration?
advanced.explanation: using x = resource automatically calls x[Symbol.dispose]() when out of scope.

advanced.question: What is the await using declaration?
advanced.explanation: await using x = asyncResource calls x[Symbol.asyncDispose]() asynchronously.

advanced.question: What is the Node.js event loop phases?
options: timers -> I/O callbacks -> idle -> poll -> check -> close
advanced.explanation: The event loop has 6 phases; each phase has a FIFO queue of callbacks.

advanced.question: What is the poll phase?
options: Retrieves new I/O events and executes callbacks
advanced.explanation: The poll phase blocks and waits for I/O if no timers are pending.

advanced.question: What is the check phase?
advanced.explanation: The check phase runs after poll if setImmediate() is scheduled.

advanced.question: What is the close phase?
advanced.explanation: Close callbacks (socket.on("close", ...)) fire in this phase.

advanced.question: What is libuv?
options: C library for async I/O in Node.js
advanced.explanation: libuv provides the event loop, thread pool, and async I/O across platforms.

advanced.question: What is the thread pool?
options: libuv pool for async file/DNS operations
advanced.explanation: Default 4 threads handle async file system and some DNS operations.

advanced.question: What is UV_THREADPOOL_SIZE?
advanced.explanation: process.env.UV_THREADPOOL_SIZE = "8" changes pool from default 4.

advanced.question: What are async hooks?
options: API tracking lifetime of async resources
advanced.explanation: async_hooks.createHook() enables monitoring async operations for diagnostics.

advanced.question: What is the diagnostic_channel?
advanced.explanation: diagnostic_channel enables tooling to observe application behavior.

beginner.question: What is Vue.js?
options: Progressive JavaScript framework for building UIs
beginner.explanation: Vue is incrementally adoptable; core library focuses on view layer.

beginner.question: What is Vue instance?
options: Root of Vue app: new Vue({ el: "#app", data: {...} })
beginner.explanation: Vue instance manages data, lifecycle, methods, computed, watchers.

beginner.question: What is template syntax?
options: HTML-like with directives: {{ message }}, v-bind, v-for
beginner.explanation: Vue templates compile to render functions; support expressions.

beginner.question: What is v-bind?
options: Binds attribute to expression: v-bind:src="url" or :src="url"
beginner.explanation: v-bind dynamically binds HTML attributes to component data.

beginner.question: What is v-model?
beginner.explanation: v-model creates value/input binding for form elements and components.

beginner.question: What is v-if?
beginner.explanation: v-if removes/creates DOM element; v-show toggles display:none.

beginner.question: What is v-show?
beginner.explanation: v-show always renders; toggles CSS display property.

beginner.question: What is v-for?
options:
List rendering:
  • {{ item }}
beginner.explanation: v-for supports :key for efficient updates; can destructure (item, index).

beginner.question: What is v-on?
beginner.explanation: v-on:click.prevent stops default; v-on:keyup.enter catches enter key.

beginner.question: What is computed?
options: Reactive computed properties cached until deps change
beginner.explanation: computed: { fullName() { return this.first + " " + this.last } }

beginner.question: What is watch?
beginner.explanation: watch: { question(newQ) { this.fetchAnswer(newQ) } } watches single field.

beginner.question: What is a method?
options: Functions defined in methods: { add() { this.count++ } }
beginner.explanation: Methods are bound to instance; can be called from template: @click="add"

beginner.question: What is a component?
options: Reusable Vue instance with template, data, methods
beginner.explanation: Vue.component("my-comp", { template: "...", data() { return {} } })

beginner.question: What is props?
beginner.explanation: Props are one-way down; children should not mutate props directly.

beginner.question: What is $emit?
beginner.explanation: $emit triggers parent event: @event="handler($event)" receives payload.

beginner.question: What are slots?
beginner.explanation: Scoped slots: <slot :item="item">{{ item.name }}</slot> exposes data.

beginner.question: What is Vue Router?
beginner.explanation: const routes = [{ path: "/", component: Home }]; new VueRouter({ routes })

beginner.question: What is router-link?
beginner.explanation: router-link renders <a> with correct href and active CSS classes.

beginner.question: What is router-view?
beginner.explanation: router-view renders the component associated with current route.

beginner.question: What is Vuex?
beginner.explanation: Vuex: state, getters, mutations, actions; centralized store.

beginner.question: What is a mutation?
beginner.explanation: store.commit("increment") triggers mutation; devtools track mutations.

beginner.question: What is an action?
options: Async operations committing mutations: action(context) { api.call().then(r => commit("set")) }
beginner.explanation: store.dispatch("fetchData") dispatches action; actions return promises.

beginner.question: What is a getter?
options: Computed state: getters { doneTodos: state => state.todos.filter(t => t.done) }
beginner.explanation: store.getters.doneTodos accesses derived state.

beginner.question: What is Vue CLI?
options: Standard tooling for Vue development (scaffolding, dev server)
beginner.explanation: vue create my-app scaffolds project with Babel, ESLint, webpack.

beginner.question: What is single-file component?
beginner.explanation: Single-file component encapsulates markup, logic, and styles.

beginner.question: What is scoped CSS?
options: <style scoped> applies only to this component
beginner.explanation: Vue adds data-v-xxx attribute to elements and CSS selectors.

beginner.question: What are lifecycle hooks?
beginner.explanation: mounted() { this.fetchData() } runs after component inserted to DOM.

beginner.question: What is created?
options: Hook after instance created, before DOM mount
beginner.explanation: created() can access data, methods, but no DOM (this.$el not available).

beginner.question: What is mounted?
options: Hook after DOM inserted: DOM access, third-party libs
beginner.explanation: mounted() has this.$el in DOM; for DOM manipulation and integrations.

beginner.question: What is beforeDestroy?
beginner.explanation: beforeDestroy() clear timers, remove event listeners, cancel subscriptions.

beginner.question: What is keep-alive?
options: Caches component state (not destroyed when toggled)
beginner.explanation: <keep-alive> <component :is="view"> </keep-alive> caches inactive.

beginner.question: What is dynamic component?
beginner.explanation: :is binds component name or options object.

beginner.question: What is async component?
beginner.explanation: Async components code-split; loaded on demand reduces bundle size.

beginner.question: What is error boundary?
beginner.explanation: errorCaptured(err, child, info) prevents error propagation.

beginner.question: What is provide/inject?
options: Dependency injection: provide() returns object; inject: ["key"]
beginner.explanation: Provide/inject avoids prop drilling; not reactive by default.

beginner.question: What is Vue.observable?
options: Makes object reactive: const state = Vue.observable({ count: 0 })
beginner.explanation: Vue.observable creates minimal reactive object without full component.

beginner.question: What is nextTick?
options: Deferred callback after DOM update: this.$nextTick(() => { ... })
beginner.explanation: nextTick ensures DOM reflects reactive changes before callback.

beginner.question: What is $refs?
beginner.explanation: <input ref="myInput"> then this.$refs.myInput accesses DOM element.

beginner.question: What is mixin?
options: Reusable options: { data(), methods, created() } mixed into component
beginner.explanation: Mixins can conflict; merge strategy overwrites or combines arrays.

beginner.question: What is custom directive?
beginner.explanation: v-focus custom directive: bind, inserted, update, unbind hooks.

beginner.question: What is filter?
options: Text formatting in template: {{ price | currency }}
beginner.explanation: Filters: Vue.filter("currency", v => "$" + v.toFixed(2)) (Vue 2).

beginner.question: What is transition?
beginner.explanation: Transition classes: .fade-enter-active, .fade-leave-active, etc.

beginner.question: What is transition-group?
beginner.explanation: transition-group adds v-move class for FLIP animation on reorder.

beginner.question: What is render function?
options: JavaScript render function: render(h) { return h("div", "hello") }
beginner.explanation: Render function bypasses template compiler; gives full JS power.

beginner.question: What is JSX in Vue?
beginner.explanation: @vue/babel-preset-jsx enables JSX in Vue components.

beginner.question: What is the virtual DOM?
options: Lightweight JS representation of real DOM
beginner.explanation: Vue patches virtual DOM differences efficiently (diff algorithm).

beginner.question: What is reactivity in Vue?
beginner.explanation: Vue 2: Object.defineProperty; Vue 3: Proxy for deep reactivity.

beginner.question: What is Vue DevTools?
options: Browser extension for debugging Vue apps
beginner.explanation: DevTools shows component tree, state, events, Vuex, routing.

beginner.question: What is Nuxt.js?
options: Vue framework for SSR, SSG, file-based routing
beginner.explanation: Nuxt enables server-side rendering, static generation, auto-imports.

beginner.question: What is Vite?
options: Next-gen build tool: fast dev server, HMR, Rollup build
beginner.explanation: Vite serves native ES modules in dev; Rollup-based production build.

intermediate.question: What is Composition API?
options: setup() function organizing logic by feature
intermediate.explanation: Composition API: setup() { const count = ref(0); const inc = () => count.value++; return { count, inc } }

intermediate.question: What is ref()?
options: Reactive primitive wrapper: const count = ref(0)
intermediate.explanation: count.value accesses/modifies; template auto-unwraps.

intermediate.question: What is reactive()?
options: Reactive object: const state = reactive({ count: 0 })
intermediate.explanation: reactive makes object deeply reactive; access without .value.

intermediate.question: What is computed()?
options: Computed ref: const double = computed(() => count.value * 2)
intermediate.explanation: computed returns readonly ref; automatically tracks dependencies.

intermediate.question: What is watch()?
intermediate.explanation: watch allows side effects on reactive changes; supports deep/watchEffect.

intermediate.question: What is watchEffect()?
options: Runs function immediately; tracks deps automatically
intermediate.explanation: watchEffect(() => console.log(count.value)) auto-tracks all refs used.

intermediate.question: What is onMounted()?
options: Lifecycle hook in Composition API: onMounted(() => fetchData())
intermediate.explanation: onMounted registers callback when component is mounted to DOM.

intermediate.question: What is onUnmounted()?
intermediate.explanation: Composition API version of beforeDestroy; for teardown logic.

intermediate.question: What is toRefs()?
options: Converts reactive object to plain refs: const { count } = toRefs(state)
intermediate.explanation: toRefs preserves reactivity when destructuring reactive objects.

intermediate.question: What is shallowRef()?
options: Ref that only tracks .value changes, not deep
intermediate.explanation: shallowRef for large objects where deep reactivity is unnecessary overhead.

intermediate.question: What is triggerRef()?
intermediate.explanation: triggerRef manually triggers effects depending on shallowRef.

intermediate.question: What is customRef()?
options: Custom ref with explicit get/set control
intermediate.explanation: customRef((track, trigger) => ({ get() { track(); return val }, set(newVal) { val = newVal; trigger() } }))

intermediate.question: What is provide/inject in Composition API?
options: provide("key", value); const val = inject("key", default)
intermediate.explanation: provide/inject avoids prop drilling in Composition API too.

intermediate.question: What is readonly()?
options: Makes reactive object readonly: const copy = readonly(state)
intermediate.explanation: readonly prevents mutations; TypeScript type is DeepReadonly.

intermediate.question: What is shallowReadonly()?
intermediate.explanation: shallowReadonly makes only top-level properties readonly.

intermediate.question: What is isRef()?
options: Checks if value is ref object
intermediate.explanation: isRef(count) returns true for ref(), computed(), shallowRef().

intermediate.question: What is unref()?
options: Returns .value if ref, else value itself
intermediate.explanation: unref(x) is shorthand for isRef(x) ? x.value : x.

intermediate.question: What is Vue Router 4?
options: Vue 3 router: createRouter({ history: createWebHistory(), routes })
intermediate.explanation: createWebHistory() uses History API; createWebHashHistory() uses hash.

intermediate.question: What is navigation guard?
intermediate.explanation: router.beforeEach((to, from) => { if (!auth) return "/login" })

intermediate.question: What is route meta fields?
options: Custom metadata on route: meta: { requiresAuth: true }
intermediate.explanation: meta fields accessible in guards: to.meta.requiresAuth.

intermediate.question: What is Pinia?
intermediate.explanation: defineStore("counter", () => { const count = ref(0); function inc() { count.value++ }; return { count, inc } })

intermediate.question: What is a Pinia store?
options: defineStore returns setup function with state + actions
intermediate.explanation: const useStore = defineStore("id", setupFn); const store = useStore();

intermediate.question: What is $patch in Pinia?
options: Bulk state update: store.$patch({ name: "New", age: 30 })
intermediate.explanation: $patch merges partial state; accepts function for complex updates.

intermediate.question: What is Pinia plugin?
intermediate.explanation: Plugin adds shared logic (localStorage sync, logging, reset).

intermediate.question: What is SSR in Vue?
intermediate.explanation: renderToString(app) returns HTML string; client hydrates.

intermediate.question: What is Teleport?
options: <Teleport to="#modal"> renders content at different DOM location
intermediate.explanation: Teleport moves content to specified DOM node (modals, tooltips).

intermediate.question: What is Suspense?
options: <Suspense> handles async dependencies (async setup, nested async)
intermediate.explanation: Suspense shows fallback until all async deps resolve.

intermediate.question: What are Fragments?
options: Components can have multiple root nodes (Vue 3)
intermediate.explanation: Vue 3 template supports multiple root elements without extra div.

intermediate.question: What is defineComponent?
intermediate.explanation: defineComponent provides type inference for Options/Composition API.

intermediate.question: What is defineAsyncComponent?
intermediate.explanation: defineAsyncComponent supports loading/error/delay/timeout options.

intermediate.question: What is CSS v-bind?
intermediate.explanation: CSS v-bind binds component state to CSS values.

intermediate.question: What is effectScope?
intermediate.explanation: effectScope collects all reactive effects (watch, computed) in scope.

intermediate.question: What is getCurrentInstance?
options: Access internal component instance in setup()
intermediate.explanation: getCurrentInstance() returns internal component; use sparingly.

intermediate.question: What is h()?
intermediate.explanation: h() creates virtual node; core of render functions.

intermediate.question: What is mergeProps?
options: Merges multiple props objects (class, style, handlers)
intermediate.explanation: mergeProps(base, overrides) merges class/style arrays, event handlers.

intermediate.question: What is resolveComponent?
intermediate.explanation: resolveComponent("MyComp") returns component options from registry.

intermediate.question: What is withDirectives?
intermediate.explanation: withDirectives(h("div"), [[vFocus, true]]) applies v-focus directive.

intermediate.question: What is Transition in Vue 3?
intermediate.explanation: Vue 3 renamed v-enter to v-enter-from, v-leave to v-leave-from.

intermediate.question: What is TransitionGroup?
options: TransitionGroup for list animations with FLIP
intermediate.explanation: TransitionGroup renders Fragment; each child needs unique key.

intermediate.question: What is KeepAlive?
intermediate.explanation: KeepAlive with include/exclude props to selectively cache.

intermediate.question: What is defineEmits?
options: Type-safe emits: const emit = defineEmits<{ (e: "update", id: number): void }>()
intermediate.explanation: defineEmits provides type-checked emit function.

intermediate.question: What is defineProps?
options: Type-safe props: const props = defineProps<{ title: string }>()
intermediate.explanation: defineProps enables type inference without runtime overhead.

intermediate.question: What is withDefaults?
options: Default values for defineProps: withDefaults(defineProps<Props>(), { count: 0 })
intermediate.explanation: withDefaults provides fallback values for optional props.

intermediate.question: What is useSlots/useAttrs?
options: Access slots and attrs in setup: const slots = useSlots()
intermediate.explanation: useSlots() and useAttrs() replace this.$slots/this.$attrs in setup.

intermediate.question: What exposes?
options: defineExpose({ ref, method }) exposes to parent template ref
intermediate.explanation: defineExpose controls what parent can access via template ref.

intermediate.question: What is VueUse?
options: Collection of Composition API utilities: useMouse, useStorage
intermediate.explanation: VueUse: useLocalStorage, useEventListener, useIntersectionObserver.

intermediate.question: What is vue-query (TanStack Query)?
options: Server state management: fetching, caching, sync
intermediate.explanation: useQuery(["users"], fetchUsers) provides loading/error/data refs.

intermediate.question: What is Express.js?
intermediate.explanation: Express provides routing, middleware, and HTTP utilities for web apps.

intermediate.question: What is middleware in Express?
options: Functions processing requests before route handler
intermediate.explanation: Middleware (app.use()) modifies req/res, ends request, or calls next().

intermediate.question: What is routing in Express?
intermediate.explanation: app.get("/users", handler), app.post("/users", handler) define routes.

advanced.question: What is Vue 3 Proxy-based reactivity?
options: Proxy allows trapping on add/delete (not possible in Vue 2)
advanced.explanation: Vue 3 uses Proxy for reactive(); supports Map, Set, adding/deleting keys.

advanced.question: What is ref() implementation?
options: ref uses reactive proxy wrapping { value: actualValue }
advanced.explanation: ref(0) creates { value: 0 } wrapped in reactive proxy.

advanced.question: What is the reactive scheduler?
options: Queues effects in microtask; deduplicates watchers
advanced.explanation: Vue batches updates; nextTick flushes queue as microtask.

advanced.question: What is the virtual DOM patching?
advanced.explanation: Vue's patch: same node? patch children; else replace.

advanced.question: What is patchFlag?
options: Compile-time optimization hint for VNode diffing
advanced.explanation: Patch flags tell renderer exactly what changed: TEXT, CLASS, STYLE, PROPS.

advanced.question: What is dynamicChildren?
options: Vue 3 optimization: only track children with dynamic bindings
advanced.explanation: Block tree tracks dynamic nodes; skip static parts during update.

advanced.question: What is hoisting?
options: Compile-time: hoist static VNodes outside render function
advanced.explanation: Hoisted VNodes created once; reused across re-renders for perf.

advanced.question: What is cacheHandlers?
options: Caches inline event handlers to avoid re-render
advanced.explanation: Inline @click="() => count++" is cached; handler reference stable.

advanced.question: What is tree-shaking?
options: Dead code elimination: unused features (v-model, Transition) not in bundle
advanced.explanation: Vue 3 core is tree-shakable; unused APIs removed by bundler.

advanced.question: What is custom renderer?
options: createRenderer({ createElement, insert, remove }) for custom targets
advanced.explanation: Custom renderer targets canvas, WebGL, or native mobile via same API.

advanced.question: What is vue-template-compiler?
advanced.explanation: Vue 3 uses @vue/compiler-sfc, which uses @vue/compiler-dom/@vue/compiler-core.

advanced.question: What is @vue/compiler-sfc?
options: Compiles .vue files: template -> render, script -> ts, style -> css
advanced.explanation: @vue/compiler-sfc parses .vue; returns descriptor with template/script/style.

advanced.question: What is the Babel plugin JSX?
advanced.explanation: JSX plugin supports v-model, slots, directives via Babel transforms.

advanced.question: What is Vite plugin for Vue?
advanced.explanation: Plugin handles SFC compilation, HMR, and template optimization.

advanced.question: What is Vitest?
options: Vite-native test runner: fast, Jest-compatible API
advanced.explanation: Vitest provides describe/it/expect; integrates with @vue/test-utils.

advanced.question: What is @vue/test-utils?
options: Test utility: mount(MyComp), shallowMount, wrapper API
advanced.explanation: const wrapper = mount(MyComp, { props: { msg: "Hello" } }); expect(wrapper.text()).toContain("Hello")

advanced.question: What is Vue Testing Library?
advanced.explanation: render(MyComp); screen.getByText("submit").click(); asserts behavior.

advanced.question: What is hydration?
options: Client-side activation of SSR rendered markup
advanced.explanation: hydrate() attaches event listeners to existing SSR DOM.

advanced.question: What is SSR hydration mismatch?
options: Differences between server HTML and client VDOM
advanced.explanation: Mismatch causes full re-render; avoid browser-only APIs in SSR.

advanced.question: What is useSSRContext?
options: Access SSR context for injecting head, state, etc.
advanced.explanation: useSSRContext() in setup provides access to render context object.

advanced.question: What is onServerPrefetch?
options: SSR data fetching hook (Nuxt/Vue Router SSR)
advanced.explanation: onServerPrefetch(async () => { await store.fetchData() }) runs during SSR.

advanced.question: What is serializer/dehydrate?
options: Serializes Vuex/Pinia state into HTML for client hydration
advanced.explanation: __INITIAL_STATE__ script tag embeds server state for client hydration.

advanced.question: What is renderToString?
options: Renders Vue app to HTML string for SSR
advanced.explanation: renderToString(app).then(html => res.send(html)) from @vue/server-renderer.

advanced.question: What is renderToNodeStream?
options: Streams SSR output for TTFB optimization
advanced.explanation: renderToNodeStream(app).pipe(res) sends HTML in chunks.

advanced.question: What is useHead in Vue?
options: Manages document head (title, meta, link) in Vue 3
advanced.explanation: useHead({ title: "My Page", meta: [{ name: "description", content: "..." }] })

advanced.question: What is @unhead/vue?
options: Head management with SSR support and dedup
advanced.explanation: @unhead/vue powers useHead; handles ordering, deduplication, SSR.

advanced.question: What is Vue Router scroll behavior?
options: Custom scroll on navigation: scrollBehavior(to, from, savedPosition)
advanced.explanation: scrollBehavior returns { top: 0 } or { el: "#section" } position.

advanced.question: What is dynamic routing in Vue Router?
advanced.explanation: addRoute adds routes after initialization for auth-based routing.

advanced.question: What is route removal?
advanced.explanation: removeRoute cleans up dynamically added routes.

advanced.question: What is NavigationFailure?
options: Router failure types: aborted, duplicated, cancelled
advanced.explanation: isNavigationFailure(failure, NavigationFailureType.duplicated).

advanced.question: What is Pinia plugin for persistence?
advanced.explanation: Persisted state plugin saves state to localStorage; restores on app load.

advanced.question: What is Pinia hydration?
options: Replaces store state with SSR/dehydrated state
advanced.explanation: pinia.state.value[storeId] = __INITIAL_STATE__[storeId] for SSR.

advanced.question: What is storeToRefs?
options: Extracts reactive refs from Pinia store
advanced.explanation: storeToRefs(store) returns refs for state/getters; avoids reactivity loss.

advanced.question: What is $subscribe in Pinia?
options: Subscribes to state mutations: store.$subscribe((mutation, state) => ...)
advanced.explanation: $subscribe fires on every state change; useful for persistence.

advanced.question: What is $onAction in Pinia?
options: Intercepts actions: store.$onAction(({ name, args, after, onError }) => ...)
advanced.explanation: $onAction hooks before/after action execution for logging/analytics.

advanced.question: What is Vue Router data fetching?
options: Route guard fetching: beforeRouteEnter/Update fetches data
advanced.explanation: beforeRouteEnter(to, from, next) { fetchData().then(d => next(vm => vm.data = d)) }

advanced.question: What is the Composition API lifecycle order?
options: setup -> onBeforeMount -> onMounted -> onBeforeUpdate -> onUpdated -> onBeforeUnmount -> onUnmounted
advanced.explanation: Composition lifecycle hooks register during setup execution.

advanced.question: What is effect cleanup?
options: onCleanup in watchEffect: watchEffect((onCleanup) => { onCleanup(() => cancel()) })
advanced.explanation: onCleanup registers cancellation callback when effect re-runs or disposes.

advanced.question: What is shallowReactive?
options: Reactive at root only: shallowReactive({ nested: { count: 0 } })
advanced.explanation: Top-level reactivity; nested objects are not deeply proxied.

advanced.question: What is markRaw?
options: Opts object out of reactivity: const obj = markRaw({ count: 0 })
advanced.explanation: markRaw prevents Proxy wrapping for performance or third-party objects.

advanced.question: What is toRaw?
options: Returns raw original object from reactive proxy
advanced.explanation: toRaw(reactiveObj) returns underlying non-reactive object.

advanced.question: What is deferred recursion?
options: Vue 3 defers deep reactive proxy creation until accessed
advanced.explanation: Proxy handlers create nested proxies lazily on property access.

advanced.question: What is the compiler optimization strategy?
options: Analyzes template: static/dynamic parts, slot compilation
advanced.explanation: Compiler marks hoisted statics, patch flags, dynamic children with ease.

advanced.question: What is the block tree?
options: Vue 3 compiler flattens dynamic nodes into array for skip
advanced.explanation: Block root collects all dynamic children; diff skips static siblings.

advanced.question: What is v-memo?
options: Vue 3.2+: memoizes template part: v-memo="[dep1, dep2]"
advanced.explanation: v-memo skips diff of subtree if deps unchanged; for heavy lists.

advanced.question: What is defineCustomElement?
options: Creates native Web Component from Vue component
advanced.explanation: defineCustomElement(MyComp) registers <my-comp> custom element.

advanced.question: What is Vue Web Components?
options: Vue components as native Web Components via defineCustomElement
advanced.explanation: Vue Web Component wraps in Shadow DOM with scoped styles.

advanced.question: What is nitro?
options: Nitro is the Nuxt 3 server engine (cross-platform, file-based)
advanced.explanation: Nitro supports Node, Deno, serverless, edge; auto-imports server routes.

advanced.question: What is unplugin?
advanced.explanation: unplugin-vue-components auto-imports components; unplugin-auto-import auto-imports APIs.

advanced.question: What is the Node.js event loop phases?
options: timers -> I/O callbacks -> idle -> poll -> check -> close
advanced.explanation: The event loop has 6 phases; each phase has a FIFO queue of callbacks.

beginner.question: What is JAMstack?
options: Modern web architecture: JavaScript, APIs, Markup
beginner.explanation: JAMstack serves pre-built markup enhanced with JS and APIs.

beginner.question: What is a static site generator?
options: Generates HTML at build time (Gatsby, 11ty, Next.js)
beginner.explanation: SSG pre-renders pages; no server needed at request time.

beginner.question: What is a headless CMS?
options: Content management system with API-only delivery
beginner.explanation: Headless CMS provides content via REST/GraphQL; no frontend coupling.

beginner.question: What is Gatsby?
options: React-based SSG with GraphQL data layer
beginner.explanation: Gatsby compiles React components to static HTML with data from multiple sources.

beginner.question: What is 11ty (Eleventy)?
beginner.explanation: 11ty supports Nunjucks, Liquid, Markdown; zero-config, flexible.

beginner.question: What is Contentful?
beginner.explanation: Contentful provides content modeling, asset management, localization.

beginner.question: What is Netlify?
options: JAMstack hosting with CI/CD, serverless functions
beginner.explanation: Netlify builds from git, deploys CDN, runs serverless functions.

beginner.question: What is Vercel?
options: Frontend cloud with serverless deployment and edge functions
beginner.explanation: Vercel deploys from git; optimized for Next.js and static sites.

beginner.question: What is a serverless function?
options: Single-purpose function running on cloud (AWS Lambda)
beginner.explanation: Serverless functions auto-scale; pay per invocation (cold start).

beginner.question: What is a CDN?
options: Content Delivery Network distributes assets globally
beginner.explanation: CDN caches static files at edge nodes for fast global delivery.

beginner.question: What is pre-rendering?
options: Generating HTML at build or request time
beginner.explanation: Pre-rendering produces HTML before browser receives it (SSG/SSR).

beginner.question: What is hydration in JAMstack?
options: Client JS attaching event handlers to static HTML
beginner.explanation: Hydration makes static HTML interactive with client-side JS.

beginner.question: What is GraphQL?
options: Query language for APIs (request only needed data)
beginner.explanation: GraphQL: query { user(id: 1) { name email } } returns requested fields.

beginner.question: What is Apollo Client?
options: GraphQL client for React/Vue/Angular with caching
beginner.explanation: Apollo Client manages local/remote data with normalized cache.

beginner.question: What is a REST API?
beginner.explanation: REST uses resource endpoints: GET /users/1, POST /users.

beginner.question: What is ISR?
beginner.explanation: ISR re-generates pages on demand while serving stale HTML.

beginner.question: What is DPR?
beginner.explanation: DPR renders on first request, persists; subsequent requests serve cached.

beginner.question: What is a webhook?
beginner.explanation: On new blog post, CMS sends POST to SSG webhook to rebuild.

beginner.question: What is Git-based deployment?
options: Push to git triggers build and deploy
beginner.explanation: Netlify/Vercel auto-deploy on git push; preview for branches.

beginner.question: What is atomic deploy?
beginner.explanation: Netlify: new deploy replaces old instantly; instant rollback.

beginner.question: What is splitting?
options: Loading JS chunks on demand (code splitting)
beginner.explanation: Dynamic import() splits code; per-page JS in JAMstack.

beginner.question: What is edge computing?
options: Running code at CDN edge nodes near user
beginner.explanation: Edge functions (CloudFlare Workers, Vercel Edge) execute near user.

beginner.question: What is Edge Functions?
options: Lightweight functions at edge: modify response, A/B test
beginner.explanation: Edge functions intercept request/response close to user location.

beginner.question: What is Lambda@Edge?
options: AWS Lambda functions at CloudFront edge
beginner.explanation: Lambda@Edge customizes CloudFront content (origin, viewer request/response).

beginner.question: What is ISG?
beginner.explanation: ISG generates pages on-demand after initial build, caching result.

beginner.question: What is Instant On?
options: Gatsby: service worker + preload for instant navigation
beginner.explanation: Instant On preloads linked pages; service worker serves from cache.

beginner.question: What is Strapi?
beginner.explanation: Strapi provides admin panel, content types, and REST/GraphQL APIs.

beginner.question: What is Sanity?
beginner.explanation: Sanity has GROQ query language, portable text, custom input components.

beginner.question: What is Prismic?
options: Headless CMS with slice-based page building
beginner.explanation: Prismic: reusable slices for building flexible layouts.

beginner.question: What is TinaCMS?
options: Git-backed headless CMS with visual editing
beginner.explanation: TinaCMS edits content in-context; saves to markdown/JSON in git.

beginner.question: What is Nuxt?
beginner.explanation: Nuxt: file-based routing, auto-imports, modules, SSR, SSG.

beginner.question: What is Next.js?
beginner.explanation: Next.js offers getStaticProps, getServerSideProps, API routes.

beginner.question: What is Redwood?
beginner.explanation: Redwood integrates React, GraphQL, Prisma, and serverless functions.

beginner.question: What is Blitz.js?
beginner.explanation: Blitz eliminates need for REST/GraphQL; direct db access from client.

beginner.question: What is Sapper?
options: Svelte framework for SSR/SSG (predecessor to SvelteKit)
beginner.explanation: Sapper: file-based routing, SSR, code-splitting for Svelte apps.

beginner.question: What is SvelteKit?
options: Svelte framework for SSR/SSG/SPA (successor to Sapper)
beginner.explanation: SvelteKit: routing, SSR, SSG, adapters (Node, Vercel, Netlify).

beginner.question: What is Astro?
options: Static site builder: zero-JS by default, islands architecture
beginner.explanation: Astro ships zero JS; interactive islands hydrate on client.

beginner.question: What is islands architecture?
options: Isolated interactive components in mostly static HTML
beginner.explanation: Islands: individual interactive components hydrate independently.

beginner.question: What is partial hydration?
options: Hydrating only interactive components, not entire page
beginner.explanation: Partial hydration sends JS only for interactive UI islands.

beginner.question: What is progressive enhancement?
options: Core content works without JS; JS enhances experience
beginner.explanation: Form submission works without JS; JS adds client-side validation.

beginner.question: What is a facade?
options: Light client-side wrapper for heavy components
beginner.explanation: Facade pattern: load heavy widget in iframe; isolate application.

beginner.question: What is micro-frontend?
options: Independent frontend apps integrated on page
beginner.explanation: Module federation: separate deploy units compose single page.

beginner.question: What is module federation?
options: Webpack 5: share code between separate builds at runtime
beginner.explanation: ModuleFederationPlugin exposes remote module for dynamic loading.

beginner.question: What is single-spa?
beginner.explanation: single-spa orchestrates multiple frameworks on same page.

beginner.question: What is Web Components?
options: Custom elements, Shadow DOM, HTML templates (standard)
beginner.explanation: Web Components are framework-neutral, native browser components.

beginner.question: What is Lit?
options: Simple library for building Web Components
beginner.explanation: Lit: @property, @event, lit-html for efficient Web Components.

beginner.question: What is Stencil?
beginner.explanation: Stencil compiles to standards-compliant Web Components with lazy loading.

beginner.question: What is TypeScript in JAMstack?
options: Type safety for build-time generated code
beginner.explanation: TypeScript catches errors at compile time; emits JS for JAMstack.

beginner.question: What is ESLint?
options: JavaScript linting: find and fix code problems
beginner.explanation: ESLint enforces code quality rules; integrates with most editors.

beginner.question: What is Prettier?
beginner.explanation: Prettier formats JS, CSS, HTML, Markdown on save or commit.

intermediate.question: What is OAuth?
intermediate.explanation: OAuth grants third-party limited access without sharing credentials.

intermediate.question: What is JWT?
options: JSON Web Token for stateless authentication
intermediate.explanation: JWT: header.payload.signature; digitally signed, URL-safe.

intermediate.question: What is OIDC?
options: OpenID Connect: authentication layer on OAuth 2.0
intermediate.explanation: OIDC adds identity: id_token JWT with user claims.

intermediate.question: What is Auth0?
options: Authentication and authorization as a service
intermediate.explanation: Auth0 provides universal login, MFA, social connections, user management.

intermediate.question: What is Firebase Auth?
intermediate.explanation: Firebase Auth supports email/password, Google, Facebook, phone, etc.

intermediate.question: What is Supabase Auth?
intermediate.explanation: Supabase Auth: Row Level Security, social login, email/password.

intermediate.question: What is serverless database?
intermediate.explanation: Serverless: Fauna, PlanetScale, Neon, Turso; pay-per-use pricing.

intermediate.question: What is Fauna?
options: Serverless GraphQL/ document database with ACID
intermediate.explanation: Fauna: FQL (Fauna Query Language); global replication, ACID, serverless.

intermediate.question: What is PlanetScale?
intermediate.explanation: PlanetScale: branching, non-blocking schema changes, connection pooling.

intermediate.question: What is Turso?
intermediate.explanation: Turso: embedded replica at edge; primary-region architecture.

intermediate.question: What is D1?
intermediate.explanation: D1: Workers-bound serverless SQLite with read replicas globally.

intermediate.question: What is KV storage?
intermediate.explanation: KV: global low-latency key-value; for config, flags, session data.

intermediate.question: What is R2?
options: CloudFlare's S3-compatible object storage (no egress fees)
intermediate.explanation: R2: store images, videos, assets; zero egress charges.

intermediate.question: What is Edge DB?
options: Edge database close to users for low latency
intermediate.explanation: Edge DB: local replica at edge node; eventual or strong consistency.

intermediate.question: What is Stellar?
intermediate.explanation: Stellar: fast, low-cost cross-border transactions via blockchain.

intermediate.question: What is Farcaster?
intermediate.explanation: Farcaster: on-chain identity, off-chain messages (hubs).

intermediate.question: What is IPFS?
options: InterPlanetary File System: decentralized content addressing
intermediate.explanation: IPFS: content-addressed; files identified by hash, not location.

intermediate.question: What is the Filecoin?
options: Decentralized storage network incentivizing storage providers
intermediate.explanation: Filecoin: miners earn FIL for storing and serving files.

intermediate.question: What is feature flag?
options: Toggle features without redeploy: LaunchDarkly, Flagsmith
intermediate.explanation: Feature flags enable gradual rollout, A/B testing, kill switch.

intermediate.question: What is A/B testing?
options: Compare two page versions: statistical significance
intermediate.explanation: A/B test: variant A (control) vs variant B (treatment) on metrics.

intermediate.question: What is canary deploy?
intermediate.explanation: Canary routes small traffic percentage to new version; rollback if issues.

intermediate.question: What is feature branch deploy?
options: Preview deploy for every branch (Netlify, Vercel)
intermediate.explanation: Each git branch gets unique URL; test changes before merging.

intermediate.question: What is CLI deploy?
options: Deploy from command line: netlify deploy, vercel deploy
intermediate.explanation: CLI deploy with --prod flag; supports draft deploys for preview.

intermediate.question: What is instant rollback?
options: Revert to previous deploy instantly (atomic swap)
intermediate.explanation: Netlify: one-click rollback to any previous deploy; zero downtime.

intermediate.question: What is deploy context?
intermediate.explanation: ctx.PROCESS_ENV === "production" || "deploy-preview" for conditional builds.

intermediate.question: What is redirect in static hosting?
intermediate.explanation: Redirect: /* /index.html 200 for SPA fallback; /old /new 301.

intermediate.question: What is rewrite?
options: Serve different content at URL without redirect
intermediate.explanation: Rewrite: /api/* https://api.example.com/:splat 200 proxies to API.

intermediate.question: What is SPA fallback?
options: Serve index.html for all routes (client-side routing)
intermediate.explanation: Netlify: /* /index.html 200 ensures router handles all paths.

intermediate.question: What is cache invalidation?
options: Purge CDN cache when content changes
intermediate.explanation: Deploy triggers cache purge on CDN; instant update propagation.

intermediate.question: What is service worker?
options: Script running in background (cache, push, sync)
intermediate.explanation: Service worker intercepts network; caches assets for offline use.

intermediate.question: What is Workbox?
options: Library for service worker generation (precache, runtime cache)
intermediate.explanation: Workbox: precacheManifest, runtime strategies (NetworkFirst, StaleWhileRevalidate).

intermediate.question: What is offline-first?
options: App functions without internet; sync when online
intermediate.explanation: Offline-first: service worker serves cached app shell; sync in background.

intermediate.question: What is manifest.json?
options: Web app manifest: name, icons, theme_color, display
intermediate.explanation: manifest.json enables "Add to Home Screen" behavior on mobile.

intermediate.question: What is PWA?
options: Progressive Web App: installable, offline, push notifications
intermediate.explanation: PWA: manifest + service worker + HTTPS for app-like experience.

intermediate.question: What is push notification?
options: Server sends notification to user via service worker
intermediate.explanation: Push API: server push event fires service worker; shows notification.

intermediate.question: What is performance budget?
options: Limit for JS size, image weight, TTI, LCP
intermediate.explanation: Lighthouse CI fails if bundle exceeds 200KB or LCP > 2.5s.

intermediate.question: What is Core Web Vitals?
options: LCP, FID, CLS: real-user performance metrics
intermediate.explanation: LCP < 2.5s, FID < 100ms, CLS < 0.1 for good user experience.

intermediate.question: What is Tree Shaking?
options: Removes unused exports from final bundle
intermediate.explanation: Tree shaking relies on ES module static structure; dead code eliminated.

intermediate.question: What is code splitting?
options: Split bundle into chunks loaded on demand
intermediate.explanation: Dynamic import(() => import("./heavy.module")) creates separate chunk.

intermediate.question: What is critical CSS?
options: Inline above-fold CSS; load rest async
intermediate.explanation: Critical CSS: extract and inline styles for above-the-fold content.

intermediate.question: What is font-display?
options: CSS: swap, fallback, optional for FOIT control
intermediate.explanation: font-display: swap shows fallback font until custom font loads.

intermediate.question: What is lazy loading images?
options: loading="lazy" attribute defers offscreen image load
intermediate.explanation: Native lazy loading: browser loads images near viewport only.

intermediate.question: What is responsive images?
options: srcset and sizes for different viewport widths

intermediate.question: What is AVIF?
options: Modern image format: better compression than WebP/JPEG
intermediate.explanation: AVIF supports HDR, transparency; ~50% smaller than JPEG at same quality.

intermediate.question: What is WebP?
intermediate.explanation: WebP: 25-35% smaller than JPEG; supported in modern browsers.

intermediate.question: What is Brotli?
options: Better compression than gzip (JavaScript, HTML, CSS)
intermediate.explanation: Brotli: 20-30% smaller than gzip; supported by all modern browsers.

intermediate.question: What is SVG?
intermediate.explanation: SVG: inline or ; style with CSS; animate with SMIL or JS.

intermediate.question: What is SEO in JAMstack?
options: Pre-rendered HTML is SEO-friendly by default
intermediate.explanation: SSG generates full HTML; search engines index content easily.

intermediate.question: What is structured data?
options: JSON-LD schema.org markup for rich snippets
intermediate.explanation: JSON-LD: {"@context": "https://schema.org", "@type": "Article", ...}

intermediate.question: What is Open Graph?
options: OG meta tags for social media previews
intermediate.explanation: og:title, og:description, og:image control Facebook/LinkedIn preview.

advanced.question: What is v8 isolates?
options: CloudFlare Workers run in V8 isolates (not containers)
advanced.explanation: V8 isolate: lightweight process; each request gets fresh isolate.

advanced.question: What is Worker lifespan?
advanced.explanation: Workers are ephemeral; no persistent memory between requests.

advanced.question: What is Durable Objects?
advanced.explanation: Durable Objects: single-instance coordination, consistent storage at edge.

advanced.question: What is WebSocket in Workers?
options: Workers can upgrade to WebSocket connection
advanced.explanation: Workers: WebSocket handshake; relay to Durable Object for state.

advanced.question: What is WaitUntil?
options: Extends Worker response: ctx.waitUntil(promise) after response
advanced.explanation: waitUntil allows background tasks (logging, analytics) after response.

advanced.question: What is Graceful shutdown?
options: Workers: WaitUntil allows tasks after returning response
advanced.explanation: Graceful: response sent; background tasks still run via waitUntil.

advanced.question: What is Composition in serverless?
options: Function composition: auth -> validate -> handler -> response
advanced.explanation: Compose: const composed = compose([auth, validate, handler]);

advanced.question: What is cold start?
options: Delay when serverless function initializes first time
advanced.explanation: Cold start: load runtime, initialize code; optimize with smaller bundles.

advanced.question: What is provisioned concurrency?
options: Lambda: pre-warmed instances avoid cold starts
advanced.explanation: Provisioned concurrency: X instances always warm; pay for reserved.

advanced.question: What is SnapStart?
options: Lambda: snapshot after init, restore for faster cold start
advanced.explanation: SnapStart: ~1s cold start -> ~200ms by restoring pre-init snapshot.

advanced.question: What is serverless.yml?
options: Serverless Framework config: functions, events, resources
advanced.explanation: serverless.yml deploys functions, API Gateway, DynamoDB tables.

advanced.question: What is AWS SAM?
advanced.explanation: SAM: AWS::Serverless::Function transforms to CloudFormation.

advanced.question: What is SST?
options: Serverless Stack Framework: CDK-based with live Lambda dev
advanced.explanation: SST: sst start deploys to AWS; Lambda updates in 1s during dev.

advanced.question: What is Arc.codes?
advanced.explanation: Arc: @http, @tables, @static; focus on infrastructure from code.

advanced.question: What is event-driven architecture?
advanced.explanation: Event-driven: SQS, SNS, EventBridge decouple microservices.

advanced.question: What is EventBridge?
options: AWS event bus for SaaS/app event routing
advanced.explanation: EventBridge: filter, transform, route events to targets (Lambda, Step Functions).

advanced.question: What is fan-out pattern?
options: SNS publishes to multiple SQS queues in parallel
advanced.explanation: SNS topic fans out event to all subscribed SQS queues concurrently.

advanced.question: What is claim check pattern?
options: Store large payload in S3; pass reference in event
advanced.explanation: Event with reference to S3: { "claimUrl": "https://bucket.s3/.../id" }

advanced.question: What is saga pattern?
options: Distributed transaction with compensating actions on failure
advanced.explanation: Saga: each step emits event; on failure, run compensating actions.

advanced.question: What is CQRS?
options: Command Query Responsibility Segregation: separate read/write models
advanced.explanation: CQRS: commands on write model; queries on read model (often denormalized).

advanced.question: What is Event Sourcing?
options: Store events as source of truth; derive current state
advanced.explanation: Event Sourcing: replay events to get state at any point in time.

advanced.question: What is GraphQL federation?
options: Compose multiple GraphQL services into single schema
advanced.explanation: @key directive defines entity; gateway resolves across subgraphs.

advanced.question: What is Apollo Federation?
advanced.explanation: Federation: each subgraph owns part of type; gateway queries all.

advanced.question: What is subgraph?
options: Independently deployed GraphQL service in federation
advanced.explanation: Subgraph declares @key on types it can resolve; gateway composes.

advanced.question: What is GraphQL Mesh?
options: Access any API (REST, gRPC, SOAP) as GraphQL
advanced.explanation: GraphQL Mesh: from OpenAPI/ gRPC to GraphQL with live config.

advanced.question: What is Step Functions?
options: AWS workflow orchestration: state machine with retry/error
advanced.explanation: Step Functions: states (Task, Choice, Parallel, Map, Wait).

advanced.question: What is AppSync?
options: AWS managed GraphQL with real-time subscriptions
advanced.explanation: AppSync: GraphQL with DynamoDB, Lambda, HTTP data sources; subscriptions via WebSocket.

advanced.question: What is Amplify?
options: AWS full-stack framework (auth, API, storage, hosting)
advanced.explanation: Amplify: graphql transforms, auth, storage, hosting, pipeline.

advanced.question: What is WebSocket scaling?
options: Broadcast to many clients: Durable Objects, SNS, Redis Pub/Sub
advanced.explanation: WebSocket scaling: connection state per node; broadcast via pub/sub.

advanced.question: What is SSE?
advanced.explanation: SSE: EventSource API auto-reconnects; text-only, unidirectional.

advanced.question: What is gRPC stream?
advanced.explanation: gRPC: client, server, bidirectional streaming over HTTP/2.

advanced.question: What is RSocket?
advanced.explanation: RSocket: multiplexed, bidirectional, reactive streams with backpressure.

advanced.question: What is tRPC?
advanced.explanation: tRPC: client imports server functions; full type safety automatically.

advanced.question: What is HATEOAS?
options: Hypermedia as Engine of App State (REST constraint)
advanced.explanation: HATEOAS: responses include links for available actions (discoverable).

advanced.question: What is API versioning?
options: URL path (/v1/...), header (Accept: vnd.api+json;v=1), or query
advanced.explanation: URL versioning: /v1/users; header versioning: Accept: version=1.

advanced.question: What is rate limiting?
options: Limit requests per IP/user: token bucket, leaky bucket
advanced.explanation: Rate limiting: 429 Too Many Requests; Retry-After header.

advanced.question: What is API monetization?
options: Charge for API usage: tiers, metering, billing
advanced.explanation: API monetization: RapidAPI, AWS Marketplace, Stripe metering.

advanced.question: What is API gateway?
options: Single entry point: auth, rate limit, caching, transformation
advanced.explanation: API Gateway (Kong, AWS API GW): aggregates multiple backend services.

advanced.question: What is service mesh?
options: Infrastructure layer for inter-service communication (mTLS, tracing)
advanced.explanation: Service mesh: sidecar proxy (Envoy, Linkerd) handles traffic management.

advanced.question: What is eBPF?
options: Extended Berkeley Packet Filter: in-kernel programmable (Cilium)
advanced.explanation: eBPF: safely extends kernel; Cilium uses eBPF for networking/security.

advanced.question: What is WebAssembly (Wasm)?
options: Portable binary: near-native speed in browser/edge/server
advanced.explanation: Wasm: compile C/Rust/Go to .wasm; execute in sandbox.

advanced.question: What is WasmEdge?
options: Edge-native Wasm runtime for serverless/stream processing
advanced.explanation: WasmEdge: fast startup (~100μs), tiny footprint, for edge computing.

advanced.question: What is CloudFlare Workers + Wasm?
options: Workers compile to Wasm for performance
advanced.explanation: Workers can use Wasm modules via import or WebAssembly.instantiate.

advanced.question: What is fastly Compute@Edge?
options: Edge computing platform using Wasm (Wizer pre-initialization)
advanced.explanation: Compute@Edge: pre-initialize Wasm with Wizer; fast cold starts.

advanced.question: What is Deno Deploy?
options: Edge runtime with V8 isolates and TypeScript natively
advanced.explanation: Deno Deploy: TypeScript first; standard Web APIs; V8 isolates.

advanced.question: What is edge config?
options: Fastly: globally distributed config store at edge
advanced.explanation: Edge Config: low-latency, globally replicated config (feature flags, redirects).

advanced.question: What is edge storage?
options: Persistence at edge with low latency (D1, KV, Turso)
advanced.explanation: Edge storage: read/write near user; global replication strategies.

advanced.question: What is session at edge?
options: Edge-cached sessions: signed cookies, KV store, cache API
advanced.explanation: Edge sessions: signed cookie with session ID; KV lookup at edge.

advanced.question: What is geolocation routing?
options: Redirect/P serve content based on user location
advanced.explanation: Workers: request.cf.country, request.cf.city for geo-aware responses.

advanced.question: What is A/B testing at edge?
options: Split traffic by cookie, header, or percentage at edge
advanced.explanation: Edge: set-Cookie variant; serve different version; report via analytics.

beginner.question: What is a bar chart used for?
What is the mean of a dataset?
What is a pie chart good for?

intermediate.question: What is the difference between population and sample?
options: Population is entire group, sample is subset
Sample is entire group, population is subset

advanced.question: What is overfitting?
options: Model fits too well to training data

advanced.question: What is the difference between supervised and unsupervised learning?

beginner.question: What is a model in ML?
What is a prediction in ML?

intermediate.question: What is a convolutional neural network (CNN)?
What is a recurrent neural network (RNN)?
What is overfitting in ML?
options: Model fits too well to training data

intermediate.question: What is a feature in ML?

advanced.question: What is a Generative Adversarial Network (GAN)?

L1 adds squared penalty, L2 adds absolute penalty
L1 for classification, L2 for regression

all.question: What is DBMS?
all.explanation: DBMS provides CRUD, transactions, concurrency, and recovery.

all.question: What is a database?
all.explanation: Databases store, retrieve, and manage persistent data efficiently.

all.question: What is a table?
options: Structured data in rows and columns
all.explanation: Tables consist of rows (records) and columns (attributes).

all.question: What is a record/row?
all.explanation: Each row represents a complete data entity.

all.question: What is a field/column?
all.explanation: Columns define the type of data: name, age, email.

all.question: What is a primary key?
all.explanation: PRIMARY KEY enforces uniqueness and not-null constraint.

all.question: What is a foreign key?
options: References primary key of another table
all.explanation: Foreign keys establish relationships between tables.

all.question: What is a candidate key?
options: Column(s) that could be primary key
all.explanation: A table may have multiple candidate keys; one is chosen as primary.

all.question: What is a composite key?
all.explanation: Composite keys use two or more columns to uniquely identify rows.

all.question: What is an index?
all.explanation: Indexes create a data structure for fast lookups; trade-off: slower writes.

all.question: What is a view?
options: Virtual table based on query result
all.explanation: Views simplify complex queries; can be updatable or read-only.

all.question: What is a materialized view?
all.explanation: Materialized views store results for faster access; need refresh.

all.question: What is a stored procedure?
options: Precompiled SQL code stored in database
all.explanation: Procedures reduce network traffic and can have business logic.

all.question: What is a trigger?
all.explanation: Triggers enforce rules, audit changes, or cascade updates.

all.question: What is ACID?
all.explanation: ACID guarantees reliable database transactions.

all.question: What is Atomicity?
options: Transaction fully completes or fully rolls back
all.explanation: All or nothing: if any part fails, entire transaction is undone.

all.question: What is Consistency?
options: Transaction brings DB from valid to valid state
all.explanation: All constraints and rules are preserved after transaction.

all.question: What is Isolation?
all.explanation: Isolation levels control visibility of uncommitted changes.

all.question: What is Durability?
options: Committed data persists even after crash
all.explanation: Committed changes survive power loss or system crashes.

all.question: What is normalization?
all.explanation: Normal forms (1NF, 2NF, 3NF, BCNF) eliminate duplication and anomalies.

all.question: What is 1NF?
all.explanation: Each column contains single value; rows are unique.

all.question: What is 2NF?
options: 1NF + no partial dependency on composite key
all.explanation: Non-key columns depend on the entire primary key, not part of it.

all.question: What is 3NF?
all.explanation: Non-key columns don't depend on other non-key columns.

all.question: What is BCNF?
options: Stronger 3NF: every determinant is a candidate key
all.explanation: BCNF resolves anomalies 3NF may still have.

all.question: What is denormalization?
all.explanation: Denormalization adds redundant data to avoid expensive joins.

all.question: What is a transaction?
options: Unit of work (one or more SQL operations)
all.explanation: BEGIN TRANSACTION; ... COMMIT or ROLLBACK.

all.question: What is COMMIT?
all.explanation: COMMIT writes transaction to database permanently.

all.question: What is ROLLBACK?
options: Undoes all changes in current transaction
all.explanation: ROLLBACK reverts database to state before the transaction.

all.question: What is a deadlock?
options: Two transactions waiting for each other's locks
all.explanation: DBMS detects deadlock and kills one transaction (victim).

all.question: What are lock types?
all.explanation: Shared locks allow reads; exclusive locks prevent all access.

all.question: What is optimistic locking?
options: Assumes no conflict; check at commit
all.explanation: Optimistic: version number or timestamp check before update.

all.question: What is pessimistic locking?
all.explanation: Pessimistic: SELECT ... FOR UPDATE prevents concurrent writes.

all.question: What is a schema?
all.explanation: Schema groups tables, views, procedures; provides namespacing.

all.question: What is a catalog?
all.explanation: Information schema or system catalog stores metadata.

all.question: What is a data dictionary?
all.explanation: Data dictionary stores schema, constraints, and access info.

all.question: What is entity-relationship (ER) model?
options: Conceptual data model with entities and relationships
all.explanation: ER diagrams show entities (rectangles) and relationships (diamonds).

all.question: What is a relationship?
all.explanation: 1:1, 1:N, M:N cardinalities define relationship types.

all.question: What is cardinality?
options: Number of instances involved in relationship
all.explanation: Cardinality: one-to-one, one-to-many, many-to-many.

all.question: What is a weak entity?
options: Entity that depends on another for existence
all.explanation: Weak entities have no key of their own; identified by parent key.

all.question: What is DDL?
options: Data Definition Language: CREATE, ALTER, DROP
all.explanation: DDL defines and modifies database structure.

all.question: What is DML?
options: Data Manipulation Language: INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE
all.explanation: DML manipulates data within existing tables.

all.question: What is DQL?
all.explanation: SELECT retrieves data from database.

all.question: What is DCL?
all.explanation: DCL manages permissions and access rights.

all.question: What is TCL?
all.explanation: TCL manages transaction boundaries.

all.question: What is a query plan?
options: Execution strategy chosen by query optimizer
all.explanation: The planner evaluates multiple strategies and picks lowest cost.

all.question: What is query optimization?
all.explanation: Query optimizer uses statistics to choose best join order and access methods.

all.question: What is a cost-based optimizer?
options: Chooses plan with lowest estimated I/O + CPU cost
all.explanation: Cost-based: use table statistics to estimate plan cost.

all.question: What are statistics in DB?
options: Histograms and row counts for optimization
all.explanation: ANALYZE TABLE updates statistics for the query optimizer.

all.question: What is a hint?
options: Forces optimizer to use specific access path
all.explanation: SELECT /*+ INDEX(t) */ * FROM t WHERE ... overrides optimizer.

all.question: What is a cluster?
all.explanation: Clustering reduces I/O for range queries on related data.

all.explanation: Weak entities have no key of their own; identified by parent key.

all.question: What is DDL?
options: Data Definition Language: CREATE, ALTER, DROP
all.explanation: DDL defines and modifies database structure.

all.question: What is DML?
options: Data Manipulation Language: INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE
all.explanation: DML manipulates data within existing tables.

all.question: What is DQL?
all.explanation: SELECT retrieves data from database.

all.question: What is DCL?
all.explanation: DCL manages permissions and access rights.

all.question: What is TCL?
all.explanation: TCL manages transaction boundaries.

all.question: What is a query plan?
options: Execution strategy chosen by query optimizer
all.explanation: The planner evaluates multiple strategies and picks lowest cost.

all.question: What is query optimization?
all.explanation: Query optimizer uses statistics to choose best join order and access methods.

all.question: What is a cost-based optimizer?
options: Chooses plan with lowest estimated I/O + CPU cost
all.explanation: Cost-based: use table statistics to estimate plan cost.

all.question: What are statistics in DB?
options: Histograms and row counts for optimization
all.explanation: ANALYZE TABLE updates statistics for the query optimizer.

all.question: What is a hint?
options: Forces optimizer to use specific access path
all.explanation: SELECT /*+ INDEX(t) */ * FROM t WHERE ... overrides optimizer.

all.question: What is a cluster?
all.explanation: Clustering reduces I/O for range queries on related data.

all.question: What is an operating system?
options: Software managing hardware and running applications
all.explanation: OS manages CPU, memory, storage, I/O, and provides user interface.

all.question: What is a process?
options: Program in execution with its own memory space
all.explanation: Process has code, data, heap, stack; isolated from other processes.

all.question: What is a thread?
options: Lightweight unit within a process (shares memory)
all.explanation: Threads share process resources (memory, files); each has own stack.

all.question: What is multitasking?
all.explanation: OS switches between processes rapidly (preemptive multitasking).

all.question: What is a system call?
options: Interface between user program and OS kernel
all.explanation: System calls (open, read, write, fork) request OS services.

all.question: What is kernel?
options: Core of OS managing resources and security
all.explanation: Kernel provides process scheduling, memory mgmt, device drivers.

all.question: What is user mode vs kernel mode?
options: User: restricted; Kernel: full hardware access
all.explanation: System calls switch from user to kernel mode.

all.question: What is virtual memory?
options: Using disk as extension of RAM (paging)
all.explanation: VM allows programs larger than physical RAM; each process has own address space.

all.question: What is paging?
all.explanation: Pages map virtual addresses to physical frames; eliminates external fragmentation.

all.question: What is a page fault?
options: Accessing page not in physical memory
all.explanation: OS loads required page from disk; may evict another page.

all.question: What is demand paging?
all.explanation: Demand paging reduces memory usage and speeds process startup.

all.question: What is a page replacement algorithm?
options: Chooses which page to evict on fault
all.explanation: FIFO, LRU, Optimal, Clock algorithms for page replacement.

all.question: What is LRU?
all.explanation: LRU approximates optimal by using past access pattern.

all.question: What is thrashing?
all.explanation: Too many active processes cause constant page faults.

all.question: What is segmentation?
all.explanation: Segments correspond to logical units (code, data, stack).

all.question: What is fragmentation?
options: Wasted memory: external (gaps) or internal (unused within block)
all.explanation: Paging eliminates external fragmentation; internal fragmentation in last page.

all.question: What is a file system?
options: Organizes and stores data on storage devices
all.explanation: File system provides hierarchy of directories and files.

all.question: What is an inode?
options: Metadata structure for file (permissions, size, location)
all.explanation: Inodes store attributes and disk block pointers.

all.question: What is a directory?
options: Special file mapping names to inodes
all.explanation: Hierarchical directory structure organizes files.

all.question: What is a hard link?
options: Multiple directory entries pointing to same inode
all.explanation: Hard links share same inode; delete only removes one name.

all.question: What is a symbolic link?
options: Special file pointing to another file path
all.explanation: Symlink can cross file systems; dangling if target deleted.

all.question: What is a mount?
options: Attaching file system to directory tree
all.explanation: mount /dev/sda1 /mnt makes storage accessible at /mnt.

all.question: What is a device driver?
all.explanation: Drivers provide uniform interface to diverse hardware.

all.question: What is a buffer?
all.explanation: Buffers smooth speed differences between devices.

all.question: What is spooling?
all.explanation: Spooling overlaps I/O of one job with computation of another.

all.question: What is DMA?
options: Direct Memory Access (device to memory without CPU)
all.explanation: DMA controller transfers data directly, freeing CPU.

all.question: What is an interrupt?
options: Hardware signal pausing CPU to handle event
all.explanation: Interrupt handler saves state, services device, restores state.

all.question: What is an exception?
options: Synchronous CPU event (division by zero, page fault)
all.explanation: Exceptions are caused by the executing instruction.

all.question: What is a trap?
all.explanation: Trap switches to kernel mode to execute system call.

all.question: What is a signal?
all.explanation: Signals can be synchronous (SIGFPE) or asynchronous (SIGTERM).

all.question: What are process states?
all.explanation: Process transitions between states via scheduler actions.

all.question: What is PCB?
all.explanation: PCB stores PID, state, registers, memory info, open files.

all.question: What is context switch?
all.explanation: Context switch overhead: save old, load new PCB.

all.question: What is the scheduler?
all.explanation: Long-term (admission), medium-term (swap), short-term (CPU).

all.question: What is FCFS scheduling?
all.explanation: FCFS: simple but causes convoy effect.

all.question: What is SJF scheduling?
options: Shortest Job First (minimizes avg wait time)
all.explanation: SJF is optimal for avg wait time but requires CPU burst knowledge.

all.question: What is Round Robin scheduling?
options: Each process gets fixed time quantum
all.explanation: Round Robin is fair with small quantum; high overhead with too small.

all.question: What is priority scheduling?
all.explanation: Priority can cause starvation of low-priority processes.

all.question: What is aging?
all.explanation: Aging prevents starvation by gradually raising priority.

all.question: What is starvation?
all.explanation: Starvation occurs when scheduler always favors other processes.

all.question: What is a race condition?
options: Multiple processes access shared data concurrently
all.explanation: Race condition produces unpredictable results depending on timing.

all.question: What is a critical section?
options: Code that accesses shared resources (mutual exclusion needed)
all.explanation: Only one process may execute in its critical section at a time.

all.question: What is a mutex?
all.explanation: Mutex ensures only one thread enters critical section.

all.question: What is a semaphore?
all.explanation: Semaphore wait() decrements (blocks if 0); signal() increments.

all.question: What is a deadlock?
options: Processes waiting for each other indefinitely
all.explanation: Deadlock: mutual exclusion, hold-and-wait, no preemption, circular wait.

all.question: What is the banker's algorithm?
options: Deadlock avoidance using resource allocation state
all.explanation: Banker's checks if granting request leaves safe state.

all.question: What is a safe state?
options: Sequence where all processes can finish
all.explanation: Safe state ensures possible completion of all processes.

all.question: What is a computer network?
all.explanation: Networks enable communication, resource sharing, and internet access.

all.question: What is the Internet?
all.explanation: Internet uses TCP/IP protocol suite for universal connectivity.

all.question: What is a subnet mask?
options: Divides IP into network and host parts
all.explanation: 255.255.255.0 means first 24 bits are network portion.

all.question: What is a gateway?
all.explanation: Default gateway routes traffic to other networks.

all.question: What is DNS?
options: Domain Name System (name to IP resolution)
all.explanation: DNS translates domain.com to IP address 93.184.216.34.

all.question: What is a MAC address?
all.explanation: MAC is 48-bit (00:1A:2B:3C:4D:5E), unique per device.

all.question: What is a port number?
all.explanation: Port 80 (HTTP), 443 (HTTPS), 22 (SSH).

all.question: What is TCP?
all.explanation: TCP guarantees delivery, ordering, and error checking.

all.question: What is UDP?
all.explanation: UDP is faster but no guarantee; used for streaming, DNS.

all.question: What is a three-way handshake?
options: SYN, SYN-ACK, ACK to establish TCP connection
all.explanation: TCP requires handshake before data exchange.

all.question: What is HTTP?
options: HyperText Transfer Protocol for web communication
all.explanation: HTTP is request-response protocol underlying the World Wide Web.

all.question: What is HTTPS?
all.explanation: HTTPS encrypts all HTTP traffic with SSL/TLS.

all.question: What is an HTTP method?
options: GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, PATCH, etc.
all.explanation: GET retrieves; POST creates; PUT replaces; PATCH updates; DELETE removes.

all.question: What is HTTP status 200?
all.explanation: 200 means request succeeded; 201 Created for POST.

all.question: What is HTTP status 404?
all.explanation: 404 means requested resource does not exist.

all.question: What is HTTP status 500?
all.explanation: 500 indicates the server encountered an unexpected condition.

all.question: What is a cookie?
options: State data stored by browser and sent with requests
all.explanation: Cookies enable session tracking and personalization.

all.question: What is a session?
all.explanation: Session identified by ID stored in cookie or URL.

all.question: What is a proxy server?
all.explanation: Proxies provide caching, anonymity, filtering, and load balancing.

all.question: What is a firewall?
options: Filters network traffic based on rules
all.explanation: Firewall blocks/permits traffic by IP, port, protocol.

all.question: What is NAT?
options: Network Address Translation (maps private to public IP)
all.explanation: NAT allows multiple devices to share one public IP.

all.question: What is DHCP?
options: Dynamic Host Configuration Protocol (auto IP assignment)
all.explanation: DHCP server assigns IP, subnet, gateway, DNS automatically.

all.question: What is ARP?
options: Address Resolution Protocol (IP to MAC mapping)
all.explanation: ARP broadcasts "who has IP 192.168.1.1?" to find MAC address.

all.question: What is ICMP?
all.explanation: ICMP reports network errors and sends echo requests (ping).

all.question: What is ping?
options: Tests network connectivity using ICMP echo
all.explanation: ping sends ICMP Echo Request; receives Echo Reply.

all.question: What is traceroute?
options: Traces path packets take to destination
all.explanation: Traceroute uses TTL (Time To Live) to discover each hop.

all.question: What is a router?
all.explanation: Router uses routing table to determine next hop.

all.question: What is a switch?
all.explanation: Switch sends frames only to intended MAC address (unlike hub).

all.question: What is a hub?
options: Repeats signals to all ports (dumb device)
all.explanation: Hub operates at Layer 1; all devices see all traffic.

all.question: What is a bridge?
all.explanation: Bridge forwards between segments based on MAC addresses.

all.question: What is OSI model?
options: 7-layer network model: Physical, Data Link, Network, Transport, Session, Presentation, Application
all.explanation: OSI standardizes network communication into 7 layers.

all.question: What is the physical layer?
options: Raw bit transmission over physical medium
all.explanation: Layer 1: cables, radio, voltage, timing, connectors.

all.question: What is the data link layer?
options: Frames between adjacent nodes (MAC, error detection)
all.explanation: Layer 2: Ethernet, Wi-Fi frames. Address via MAC.

all.question: What is the network layer?
all.explanation: Layer 3: IP addressing, routing, packet fragmentation.

all.question: What is the transport layer?
all.explanation: Layer 4: port numbers, segmentation, flow control.

all.question: What is the session layer?
all.explanation: Layer 5: session establishment, maintenance, termination.

all.question: What is the presentation layer?
all.explanation: Layer 6: data format conversion (SSL/TLS, JPEG, ASCII).

all.question: What is the application layer?
all.explanation: Layer 7: applications use network services.

all.question: What is TCP/IP model?
all.explanation: TCP/IP is the practical internet model with fewer layers.

all.question: What is an IP packet?
options: Data unit at network layer (header + payload)
all.explanation: IP packet contains source/dest IP, TTL, protocol, data.

all.question: What is a TCP segment?
options: Data unit at transport layer (TCP header + data)
all.explanation: TCP segment includes sequence number, ack, ports, flags.

all.question: What is a socket?
all.explanation: Socket (192.168.1.1:80) uniquely identifies a connection endpoint.

all.question: What is latency?
options: Time for data to travel source to destination
all.explanation: Latency depends on distance, processing, queuing, propagation.

all.question: What is bandwidth?
all.explanation: Bandwidth measured in bps; higher bandwidth = more data per second.

all.question: What is throughput?
all.explanation: Throughput <= bandwidth; affected by congestion and overhead.

all.question: What is packet loss?
all.explanation: Packet loss causes retransmission and degraded performance.

all.question: What is jitter?
all.explanation: Jitter affects real-time applications like VoIP and gaming.

all.question: What is QoS?
all.explanation: QoS gives priority to critical traffic (voice, video).

all.question: What is OOP?
options: Object-Oriented Programming paradigm based on objects
all.explanation: OOP organizes code into objects containing data and behavior.

all.question: What is a class?
all.explanation: A class defines the structure and behavior that objects will have.

all.question: What is an object?
options: Instance of a class with state and behavior
all.explanation: An object is a concrete entity created from a class template.

all.question: What is encapsulation?
options: Hiding internal state; exposing via methods
all.explanation: Encapsulation bundles data and methods; prevents direct external access.

all.question: What is data hiding?
options: Restricting direct access to internal data
all.explanation: Private/protected members hide implementation details from outside.

all.question: What is abstraction?
all.explanation: Abstraction hides implementation; classes/methods expose only necessary interface.

all.question: What is inheritance?
all.explanation: Inheritance: class Dog extends Animal; reuses and extends parent behavior.

all.question: What is single inheritance?
options: Class inherits from one parent class
all.explanation: Java, C#: single inheritance; C++ allows multiple inheritance.

all.question: What is multiple inheritance?
